title: Suspected poisonous mushroom exposure information card
summary: A no-delay information card for mushroom ingestion that preserves samples and meal details, contacts poison expertise, and avoids inducing vomiting or waiting for symptoms.
content:
A no-delay information card for mushroom ingestion that preserves samples and meal details, contacts poison expertise, and avoids inducing vomiting or waiting for symptoms.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
materials: photos of whole mushroom and habitat
steps: Call the Greek Poison Centre or 112 promptly after suspected ingestion, especially for a child, symptoms, unknown species, or multiple people exposed.
Record who ate, estimated amount, preparation, exact times, symptoms, medicines, age and weight when known, and everyone who shared the meal.
Preserve uncooked and cooked remains separately, photograph whole specimens and the growing location, and take them only as professionals direct.

Follow professional instructions, keep the person observed, and arrange transport immediately if advised or if severe vomiting, diarrhoea, confusion, weakness, breathing problems, seizures, or collapse occur.
warnings: Do not induce vomiting, give home remedies, or wait for delayed symptoms.
Do not taste another specimen to identify it.
common_mistakes: Starting Suspected poisonous mushroom exposure information card without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Suspected poisonous mushroom exposure information card.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Suspected poisonous mushroom exposure information card when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Suspected poisonous mushroom exposure information card when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Suspected poisonous mushroom exposure information card.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Suspected poisonous mushroom exposure information card safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Suspected poisonous mushroom exposure information card when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Suspected poisonous mushroom exposure information card.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Suspected poisonous mushroom exposure information card should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Suspected poisonous mushroom exposure information card whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Suspected poisonous mushroom exposure information card in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Suspected poisonous mushroom exposure information card after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Suspected poisonous mushroom exposure information card cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Suspected poisonous mushroom exposure information card are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Suspected poisonous mushroom exposure information card to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Suspected poisonous mushroom exposure information card.']
